title: Using Index as Key – Safe or Not?
description: Understand when using array index as key is acceptable.

slides.title: The Index Trap 🪤
slides.content:
# When is index as key OK?

Using the array index as a key is **discouraged** because if the list changes (items added/removed/reordered), the index also changes, causing keys to become unstable. This can lead to:

* Unnecessary re-renders
* Buggy behavior with component state (e.g., input fields losing focus or keeping wrong values)

## When it might be safe:

* The list is static and will never change.
* The list will never be reordered or filtered.
* The items have no state (like plain text).

In most real-world apps, it's better to use a unique ID.

slides.title: The Problem Demonstrated
slides.content: Consider a list of inputs where each has internal state:

quizData.question: When is it acceptable to use array index as a key?

options.text: When the list is static and won't change
When the list has more than 10 items
When you also provide a unique id prop

quizData.explanation: If the list never changes (no additions, removals, reordering), index as key can be safe. But it's still brittle; better to use a stable ID when possible.
